name: aws-orga-deployer

metadata.name: aws-orga-deployer
metadata.summary: Deploy infrastructure-as-code at the scale of an AWS organization

documentation:
# AWS Orga Deployer

*Read the documentation: [https://malaval.github.io/aws-orga-deployer](https://malaval.github.io/aws-orga-deployer)*

*Medium story: [https://medium.com/@malavaln/why-i-built-my-own-tool-to-deploy-landing-zones-on-aws-9ed609c1cb25](https://medium.com/@malavaln/why-i-built-my-own-tool-to-deploy-landing-zones-on-aws-9ed609c1cb25)*

## Introduction

AWS Orga Deployer makes it easier to deploy and manage infrastructure-as-code at the scale of an AWS organization. It enables to deploy Terraform or CloudFormation templates and to execute Python scripts in multiple AWS accounts and multiple regions, making it particularly suitable for building AWS foundations (or Landing Zones).

To get started, develop modules (Terraform or CloudFormation templates or Python scripts), create a package definition file to specify which modules to deploy in which accounts and regions and using which parameters, and let AWS Orga Deployer deploy modules and manage dependencies between deployments.

## Installation

AWS Orga Deployer is a Python package with a command-line interface. To install it and check that it works:

```bash
pip install aws-orga-deployer
aws-orga-deployer --help
```

## Documentation

The documentation is available at [https://malaval.github.io/aws-orga-deployer](https://malaval.github.io/aws-orga-deployer). For an example of how to use AWS Orga Deployer,  read the [Getting Started](https://malaval.github.io/aws-orga-deployer/getting-started.html) page.

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.python
documentation: Engine for Python modules.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.python.Engine

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.python.Engine.validate_module_config
documentation:
Validate the content of the module configuration.

Args:
    module_config: Module configuration.

Raises:
    AssertionError

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.python.Engine.prepare
documentation:
Create a file `input.json` that contains input parameters, and return
a command that executes the Python module script in a "wrapper" function.

Args:
    key: Step key to execute.
    command: CLI command requested ("preview" or "deploy").
    action: Action to be made ("create", "update" or "destroy").
    variables: Input variables.
    module_config: Module configuration.
    deployment_cache_dir: Path to the module deployment cache directory.
    engine_cache_dir: Path to the engine cache directory (common to all
        modules for this engine).

Returns:
    List of commands to execute in subprocesses.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.python.Engine.postprocess
documentation:
Reads the file `output.json` created by the "wrapper" function and
returns the outputs.

Args:
    key: Step key to execute.
    command: CLI command requested.
    action: Action to be made ("create", "update", "destroy").
    module_config: Module configuration.
    deployment_cache_dir: Path to the module deployment cache directory.

Returns:
    Outcome of the step.
Engine for Python modules.

    engine_cache_dir: Path to the engine cache directory (common to all
        modules for this engine).

Returns:

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base
documentation: Base class inherited by engine classes.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.get_python_executable
documentation:
Return the path to the Python executable.

Args:
    module_config: Module configuration.

Returns:
    Path to the Python executable.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.StepCommand
documentation:
Details about a subprocess to execute.

Attributes:
    name: Friendly name for the subprocess
    args: List of subprocess arguments.
    cwd: Current working directory of the subprocess.
    assume_role: True if the IAM role must be assumed and AWS temporary
        credentials must be provided as environment variables to the
        subprocess.
    env: Additional environment variables to pass to the subprocess.
        Default to an empty dict (no additional environment variables).
    stdout_file: To save the content of the subprocess standard output
        `stdout` to a file, provide a file path.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.StepOutcome
documentation:
Contains the results of the step execution.

Attributes:
    made_changes: True if the step resulted in changes made or to be made.
    result: Summary of the result.
    detailed_results: Optional detailed results, such as the list of
        resources added, changed or deleted. Should only be valued for
        completed steps.
    outputs: Outputs returned when the step has completed. Should be valued
        only when the command is "apply" and if the action is not "destroy".

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine.__init__
documentation:
Create a BaseEngine instance.

Args:
    name: Name of the module
    path: Path of module files

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine._generate_module_hash
documentation: Return the MD5 hash of the module.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine._get_filename_patterns_to_hash
documentation:
Set the values of `self.included_patterns` and
`self.excluded_patterns` that define the patterns of file names to
use for computing the module hash.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine._md5_update_from_file
documentation: Update the MD5 hash with a file.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine._md5_update_from_dir
documentation:
Update the MD5 hash by browsing files and folders recursively. Only
the file names matching the patterns are used to compute the hash.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine.validate_module_config
documentation:
Validate the content of the module configuration.

Args:
    module_config: Module configuration.

Raises:
    AssertionError

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine.prepare
documentation:
Prepare inputs and return a list of commands to execute in
subprocesses.

Args:
    key: Step key to execute.
    command: CLI command requested ("preview" or "deploy").
    action: Action to be made ("create", "update" or "destroy").
    variables: Input variables.
    module_config: Module configuration.
    deployment_cache_dir: Path to the module deployment cache directory.
    engine_cache_dir: Path to the engine cache directory (common to all
        modules for this engine).

Returns:
    List of commands to execute in subprocesses.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine.postprocess
documentation:
Parse the files that are generated by the subprocesses and return
step outcomes.

Args:
    key: Step key to execute.
    command: CLI command requested.
    action: Action to be made ("create", "update", "destroy").
    module_config: Module configuration.
    deployment_cache_dir: Path to the module deployment cache directory.

Returns:
    Outcomes of the step.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine.write_wrapper_inputs
documentation:
When using a Python wrapper script, write the inputs of the `prepare`
function to a JSON file `input.json` that can be read by the wrapper.

Args:
    key: Step key to execute.
    command: CLI command requested ("preview" or "deploy").
    action: Action to be made ("create", "update" or "destroy").
    variables: Input variables.
    module_config: Module configuration.
    deployment_cache_dir: Path to the module deployment cache directory.
    engine_cache_dir: Path to the engine cache directory (common to all
        modules for this engine).

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.engines.base.BaseEngine.read_wrapper_outputs
documentation:
When using a Python wrapper script, read the outputs of the wrapper
script `output.json` and return a `StepOutcome` object.

Args:
    deployment_cache_dir: Path to the module deployment cache directory.

Returns:
    StepOutcome object.

Base class inherited by engine classes.

Attributes:
    name: Name of the module.
    engine: Name of the engine.
    module_dir: Path to the module directory.
    default_included_patterns: Default file patterns to include for
        computing the module hash of all modules for this engine, if no
        custom hash configuration is provided.
    default_excluded_patterns: Default file patterns to exclude for
        computing the module hash of all modules for this engine, if no
        custom hash configuration is provided.
    included_patterns: File patterns to include for this module (custom if
        a hash configuration file exists, or default).
    excluded_patterns: File patterns to exclude for this module (custom if
        a hash configuration file exists, or default).
    module_hash: Module hash.

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga
documentation: Information on AWS accounts and organizational units.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.__init__
documentation: Load and parse information on AWS accounts and OUs.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser._load_from_cache
documentation:
Load information on accounts and organizational units from the cache
in S3 if it exists and has not expired, and if the CLI argument
`--force-orga-refresh` is not passed.

Returns:
    True if the cache in S3 was used to load information about accounts
        and organizational units, False otherwise.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser._fetch_accounts
documentation:
Query AWS Organizations for information on AWS accounts and store it in
the attribute `accounts`.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser._fetch_ous
documentation:
Query AWS Organizations for information on organizational units and
store it in the attribute `_ous`.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.export
documentation:
Returns a dict with information on AWS accounts and organizational
units.

Returns:

: Dictionary whose structure is:

  ```
  {
      'Accounts': accounts,
      'OUs: ous
  }
  ```

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.get_all_accounts
documentation:
Return the list of all accounts IDs in the organization.

Returns:
    List of account IDs.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.get_accounts_by_id
documentation:
Return the list of accounts IDs in the organization that intersect
with a given list of account IDs.

Args:
    account_ids: List of account IDs.

Returns:
    List of account IDs.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.get_accounts_by_name
documentation:
Return the list of accounts IDs in the organization whose account
name matches one of the given patterns.

Args:
    patterns: List of account name patterns.

Returns:
    List of account IDs.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.get_accounts_by_ou
documentation:
Return the list of account IDs in the organization that belong to
at least one of the organization units in a given list.

Args:
    out_ids: List of organizational unit IDs.

Returns:
    List of account IDs.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.get_accounts_by_tag
documentation:
Return the list of account IDs in the organization that have a set of
given tags assigned.

Args:
    tags: List of tags TAG_KEY=TAG_VALUE.

Returns:
    List of account IDs.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.get_accounts_by_ou_tag
documentation:
Return the list of account IDs in the organization that belong to
at least one organizational unit that have a set of given tags assigned.

Args:
    tags: List of tags TAG_KEY=TAG_VALUE.

Returns:
    List of account IDs.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.get_account_regions
documentation:
Return the list of regions that are enabled in a given account and
that intersect with a given list of regions. `ALL_ENABLED` returns all
enabled regions.

Args:
    account_id: Account ID.
    regions: List of regions.

Returns:
    List of regions.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.get_all_enabled_regions
documentation:
Return a list of regions that are enabled in at least one account.

Return:
    List of regions.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.get_account_name
documentation:
Return the account name of a given account ID.

Args:
    account_id: Account ID

Returns:
    Account name

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.orga.OrgaParser.account_region_exists
documentation:
Return True if the account exists in the organization and the region
is enabled in this account.

Args:
    account_id: Account ID
    region: Region

Returns:
    True if the account exists and the region is enabled.

Class to retrieve and use information from AWS Organizations.

Attributes:
    accounts: Information about accounts
    ous: Information about organizational units
    loaded_from_cache: True if it was loaded from the cache in S3

Instantiate the Engine class for each module and store them in a

documentation: pylint: disable=invalid-name

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils
documentation: General utility functions.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.get_aws_temp_credentials
documentation:
Assume an IAM role and return AWS temporary credentials.

Args:
    iam_role: ARN of the IAM role to assume.
    sts_session: boto3 Session to use for the STS call. Defaut is None to
        use a new session with current AWS credentials.

Returns:
    dict: With `AccessKeyId`, `SecretAccessKey` and `SessionToken`
        attributes.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.get_aws_session
documentation:
Assume an IAM role if needed and return a boto3 Session object.

Args:
    iam_role: ARN of the IAM role to assume. Defaut is None to use current
        AWS credentials.
    sts_session: boto3 Session to use for the STS call. Defaut is None to
        use a new session with current AWS credentials.

Returns:
    Session: boto3 Session.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.get_aws_client
documentation:
Return a boto3 Client with retry configuration.

Args:
    session: boto3 Session to use.
    *args: Variable length argument list.
    **kwargs: Arbitrary keyword arguments.

Returns:
    boto3 Client object.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.exec_multithread
documentation:
Execute a function for each item of a list using concurrent threads.

Args:
    items: List of items to process.
    func: Function to use to process each item.
    concurrency: Number of concurrent threads.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.get_s3_key
documentation:
Prepends the S3 prefix to the object path and returns the result.

Args:
    object_path: Object path

Returns:
    S3Prefix concatenated with object path.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.load_json_from_s3
documentation:
Load a JSON file from S3 and return a dict.

Args:
    object_path: Object path in the dedicated S3 prefix.

Returns:
    Dictionary containing the JSON file.

Raises:
    FileNotExists: If the object does not exist in S3.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.write_dict_to_s3
documentation:
Write a dict to Amazon S3 as a JSON file.

Args:
    content: Content to wroite.
    object_path: Object path in the dedicated S3 prefix.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.write_output_json
documentation:
Write the input dictionary as a JSON file to the local disk.

Args:
    input_dict: Input content
    description: Description of the file to print in the logs

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.PropagatingThread

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.PropagatingThread.run
documentation: pylint: disable=attribute-defined-outside-init
pylint: disable=broad-exception-caught
Thread object that raises exception in the parent thread.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.utils.FileNotExists
documentation:
Exception raised if the S3 object does not exist when using
`load_dict_from_s3`.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.ModuleAccountRegionKey

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.ModuleAccountRegionKey.__init__
documentation: A dict or 3 values (module, account_id, region) can be provided.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.ModuleAccountRegionKey.to_dict
documentation: Export to a dictionary.

Hashable class that can be used as key for stores and graphes related
to module deployments.

Attributes:
    module: Module name
    account_id: Account ID
    region: Region

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.TargetDeploymentDetails

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.TargetDeploymentDetails.to_dict
documentation: Export to a dictionary.

Store details about a module deployment in the target state.

Attributes:
    variables: Variables used for this module deployment.
    var_from_outputs: Variables populated from the outputs of other module
        deployments.
    dependencies: List of module deployments that must be created before or
        deleted after this module deployment.
    module_hash: Current value of the module hash.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.CurrentDeploymentDetails

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.CurrentDeploymentDetails.__init__
documentation: A dict generated with `to_dict` or 4 values can be provided.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.CurrentDeploymentDetails.to_dict
documentation: Export to a dictionary.

Store details about a module deployment in the current state.

Attributes:
    variables: Variables used for this module deployment.
    var_from_outputs: Variables populated from the outputs of other module
        deployments.
    dependencies: List of module deployments that must be created before or
        deleted after this module deployment.
    module_hash: Value of the module hash when the deployment was made.
    outputs: Outputs returned by this module deployment.
    last_changed_time: Last time this module deployment was changed.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.CurrentStateStore

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.CurrentStateStore.save
documentation:
Save the content to S3 if it has changed since the last time it was
saved.

Returns:
    True if the package state was saved to S3, False if no change was
        found.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.store.CurrentStateStore.stop
documentation:
Force the daemon thread that saves the current stat at regular
intervals to stop.

Class that loads and saves the content of a dictionay from S3. The
keys must be of type ModuleAccountRegionKey and the values must be of type
CurrentDeploymentDetails.

Attributes:
    period: Frequency at which the package state is saved to S3. If the
        period is 0, the periodic saving is disabled.

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.schema
documentation: Define the schema of the package definition file.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.schema.validate
documentation:
Validate the content provided with the schema.

Args:
    content: Dict to validate.

Raise:
    ValidationError: If the schema is invalid.

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph
documentation:
Module to represent the execution of a package as a graph, with nodes
representing the steps and each step corresponding to a module deployment,
and the edges representing dependencies between steps.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.GraphError
documentation: Exception raised when the deployment graph is invalid.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.NoMorePendingStep
documentation:
Exception raised when `next` has no steps to return because all pending
steps in a graph have been processed.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.NoProcessableStep
documentation:
Exception raised when `next` has no steps to return because all remaining
steps need to wait for their dependencies to complete.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.StepDetails
documentation:
Store details about a graph step.

Attributes:
    action: Action to make for this step. Can be "none" (no action
        to make), "create" (deployment to create), "update" (deployment
        to update), "conditional-update" (deployment to update only if the
        values of outputs on which the deployment depends change), "destroy"
        (deployment to destroy).
    skip: True if the step is skipped due to CLI arguments, or because no
        action is needed.
    status: Current step status. Can be "pending", "skipped", "ongoing",
        "completed" or "failed".
    nb_attempts: Number of times this step has been attempted.
    max_attempts: Number of times this step should be attempted before
        it is marked as "failed".
    delay: Delay in seconds to wait before retrying this step.
    wait_until: Must wait until `wait_until` because attempting to process
        this step.
    result: One-line summary of the result.
    detailed_results: Optional detailed results, such as the list of
        resources created, updated or deleted.
    made_changes: True if the step resulted in changes made or to be made

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.list_steps
documentation:
Returns the list of steps in the deployment graph.

Returns:
    List of keys.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.list_steps_with_details
documentation:
Returns the list of steps in the deployment graph and their details.

Returns:
    List of tuples (key, details).

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.get_step_details
documentation:
Return the details for a given step.

Args:
    key: Step key

Returns:
    Step details

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.has_ascendants_with_changes
documentation:
Return True if the step has ascendant dependencies on steps with
pending changes to be made.

Args:
    key: Step key

Returns:
    True if at least one ascendant step has changes to be made.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.add_step
documentation:
Add a new step in the graph, which corresponds to a module
deployment. Refers to the attributes of `StepDetails` for description.

Args:
    key: Step key
    action: Action to be made
    skip: True if the step should be skipped
    max_attempts: Maximum number of attemps for this step
    delay: Number of steps to wait before retrying

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.add_dependency
documentation:
Add a new edge in the graph, which corresponds to a dependency
between steps.

Args:
    from_key: Dependency of the module deployment.
    to_key: Module deployment
    is_var (str): True if the dependency is of type `VariablesFromOutputs`.
    ignore_if_not_exists: Ignore if the dependent deployment does not
        exist if set to True. Otherwise, raise an exception.

Raises:
    GraphError: If the dependency does not exist in the graph, unless
        the action is `delete` because we don't need to wait for the
        dependency to be deleted if it doesn't exist, or if
        `ignore_if_not_exists` is True.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.validate
documentation: Validate the graph and make initial changes.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph._check_for_loops
documentation: Check that there are no loops in the graph.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph._check_for_uncreatable_deployments
documentation:
Steps that depend on other deployments can only be created if
their ancestors have been created.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph._check_for_undeletable_deployments
documentation:
Deployments on which other deployments depend can only be deleted if
their descendants have been deleted.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph._propagate_conditional_update
documentation:
When a deployment A depends on the output values of a deployment B,
updating B may require updating A if the output values change. Repeat
until propagation is completed.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph._set_status_skipped
documentation:
Change the status of steps where no actions are needed or steps to
skip.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.next
documentation:
Return the next step to process.

Returns:
    The next step key to process.

Raises:
    aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.NoProcessableStep
    aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.NoMorePendingStep

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.complete
documentation:
Change the status of a node to "completed" and set the value
of the result.

Args:
    key: Step key
    made_changes: True if the step resulted in changes made or to be made
    result: One-line summary of the result. Default to empty.
    detailed_results: Dict with detailed results. Default to None.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.DeploymentGraph.fail
documentation:
Change the status of a node to "failed" if no further retries should
be attempted and set the value of the result.

Args:
    key: Step key.
    result: One-line summary of the result. Default to empty.
    detailed_results: Dict with detailed results. Default to None.

Class that represents the deployment steps, their dependencies and
status.

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.config
documentation: Constants or variables shared across multiple modules.

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.cli
documentation: Parse command-line interface arguments.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.cli._account_id_type
documentation: Check that the value is an account ID.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.cli._tag_type
documentation: Check that the value is in the form TAG_KEY=TAG_VALUE.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.cli._check_positive_int
documentation: Check that the argument value is an integer larger than 0.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.cli._parse_cli_args
documentation: Parse the CLI arguments and store them in the variable `config.CLI`.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.cli._create_cli_logger
documentation:
Create and return a logger for the CLI that writes to stdout.

Returns:
    CLI logger

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.cli.init_cli
documentation:
Main function to parse CLI arguments and initialize a logger.

Returns:
    CLI logger

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution
documentation: Execute the steps of a deployment package.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution.SubprocessError
documentation: Exception to raise if an error occurs when running subprocesses.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution.Executor

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution.Executor._create_temp_folders
documentation:
Create the temporary folders in the local disk to store cache files
and logs.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution.Executor.run
documentation: Function to call to launch the execution of steps.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution.Executor.worker
documentation: Main function executed by concurrent workers.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution.Executor._add_aws_temp_credentials
documentation:
Add AWS temporary credentials as environment variables to the dict
`env`.

Args:
    env (dict): Dictionary to which environment variables are added
    iam_role (str): ARN of the IAM role to assume

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution.Executor._write_subprocess_logs
documentation:
Append subprocess logs to the local disk.

Args:
    root_logs_dir: Root path of the folder that stores logs for this run
    key: Step key
    name_command: Name of the subprocess
    nb_attempts: Current attempt number
    filename: Name of the file to which logs must be appended
    content: Logs to append

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution.Executor._catch_sigint
documentation:
Catch SIGINT signals while workers are running. The first time
CTRL+C is pressed, workers must stop after completing their current
step. The second time, workers must be send a SIGINT signal to the
running subprocess and wait for a graceful exit. The third time,
workers must send a SIGTERM and wait subprocesses to exit. The fourth
time, a KeyboardInterrupt exception is raised to interrupt this
program.
pylint: disable=unused-argument
The arguments `sig` and `frame` are mandatory but unused

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.execution.Executor._stop_catching_sigint
documentation: Stop catching SIGINT.

Class that is used to execute the steps of a deployment package using
the modules.

Attributes:
    package: Current package.
    nb_times_sigint: Number of times CTRL+C was pressed.
    stop_workers: Event object used to inform worker threads that they must
        stop after completing the current step.
    send_sigint: Event object used to inform worker threads that they must
        send a SIGINT signal to their subprocesses to quit gracefully.
    send_sigint: Event object used to inform worker threads that they must
        send a SIGTERM signal to their subprocesses to force quit.
    aws_temp_credentials: Store AWS temporary credentials retrieved for each
        IAM role and when they were obtained. Avoid to do an AssumeRole for
        every step.
    lock_aws_temp_credentials: Lock object to avoid multiple threads to
        retrieve AWS temporary credentials at the same time.
    lock_next: Lock object to avoid multiple threads to retrieve the next
        step at the same time.
    engines_cache_dir: Path to the folder that contains engines cache
    deployments_cache_dir: Path to the folder that contains deployments cache
    root_logs_dir: Root path to the logs of this run

modules.name: aws_orga_deployer.package
documentation: Load the package definition file and determine the modules to deploy.

functions.name: aws_orga_deployer.package._replace_keywords
documentation:
Replace any occurences of `${CURRENT_ACCOUNT_ID}` by `current_account_id`
and of `${CURRENT_REGION}` by `current_region` recursively across lists and
dictionaries.

Args:
    source: Input object
    current_account_id (str): Value to replace `${CURRENT_ACCOUNT_ID}`
    current_region (str): Value to replace `${CURRENT_REGION}`

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.PackageError
documentation: Exception raised when the package definition file is invalid.

classes.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.__init__
documentation:
Load and validate the package definition file. This is needed to
retrieve package configuration settings, such as the name of the bucket
that stores persistent data.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.full_init
documentation:
Load additional elements needed for the CLI command `list`, `preview`
or `deploy`: Determine the module deployments that should exist in the
target state, the module deployments that currently exist by reading the
package state file, and evaluate the changes to be made.

Args:
    orga: Object to query for information on the accounts and the
        organizational units.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package._init_target_deployments
documentation:
Determine the module deployments that must exist in the target
state based on the package definition file.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package._process_deployment_block
documentation:
Process a deployment block, i.e. an item in the attribute
`Deployments` of a module in the package definition file, to determine
the expected module deployments.

Args:
    deployment_block: Content of the deployment block
    module: Module name

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package._set_variables_from_outputs
documentation:
For a given module deployment, set the value of its variables that
depend on the outputs of other module deployments.

Args:
    t_key: Step key for which variables must be updated with values
        of ascendant outputs

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package._init_cli_filters
documentation:
Identify the filters defined by the CLI arguments `--include`
and `--exclude` that restrict the modules, accounts or regions to
deploy.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package._get_retry_parameters
documentation:
Return the retry parameters for a given module from the module
configuration parameters.

Args:
    module: Module name

Returns:
    tuple:
        Maximum number of attempts. Default is 1
        Number of seconds to wait before retrying. Default is 0

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package._check_update_needed
documentation:
Return True if the module deployment must be updated because the
current and target state differs, and because the CLI argument
`--force-update` is set.

Args:
    current: Current deployment details
    target: Target deployment details

Returns:
    True if update is needed

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package._is_skipped_by_cli_filters
documentation:
Check if a given module deployment should be skipped due to the CLI
arguments.

Args:
    key: Step key.

Returns:
    True if skipped

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.next
documentation:
Retrieve the next deployment to process and the action to make.
Also update the value of the variables that eventually depends on the
outputs of other module deployments.

Returns:
    tuple:
        Step key
        Action to make. "conditional-update" is replaced by
            "skipped" or "update" depending on whether output values
            changed or not
        Current attempt number
        Maximum number of attempts

Raises:
    aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.NoProcessableStep
    aws_orga_deployer.package.graph.NoMorePendingStep

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.get_module_config
documentation:
Return the module configuration for a given module deployment. The
keywords `${CURRENT_ACCOUNT_ID}` and `${CURRENT_ACCOUNT}` are replaced
by the account ID and region of the key.

Args:
    key: Step key.

Returns:
    Module configuration parameters

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.complete
documentation:
Mark a deployment as completed, and update the current package state
if the command is "apply".

Args:
    key: Step key
    made_changes: True if the step resulted in changes made or to be made
    result: One-line summary of the result.
    detailed_results: Dict with detailed results.
    outputs: Outputs returned by the module deployment, only when the
        action is not "destroy". Default is None to store an empty dict.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.fail
documentation:
Mark a deployment as failed.

Args:
    key: Step key.
    result: One-line summary of the result.
    detailed_results: Dict with detailed results.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.update_hash
documentation:
Update the value of the module hash if the deployment action
is "update". Then, mark the deployment as completed.

Args:
    key: Step key.

Returns:
    True if the module hash was updated, False otherwise

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.save
documentation:
Save the current package state. Optionally stop the auto-save of the
current state  That is particularly useful for testing because
the auto-save might continue to write to S3 after the test has
completed.

Args:
    stop_autosave (bool): Stop the daemon thread of the current state
        store that saves the state to S3 at regular intervals.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.analyze_changes
documentation:
Display information about the changes to make and return whether
there are pending changes to be made.

Returns:
    bool: True if there are pending changes to be made.

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.export_changes
aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.analyze_results
documentation:
Display information about the results and return changes to
resources must be made or were made.

Returns:
    Tuple:
        bool: True if changes to resources have to be made or were made.
        bool: True if some steps failed

methods.name: aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.export_results
aws_orga_deployer.package.Package.remove_orphans
documentation:
Remove the orphaned module deployments from the current state, which
correspond to the module deployments for the accounts that not longer
exist in the organization, or regions that are not longer enabled in
the account.

Args:
: dry_run: Set to True to return the list of orphans without making

  : any changes.

Returns:

: list: List of dictionaries of the following structure:

  ```
  {
      "Module" (str),
      "AccountId" (str),
      "Region" (str)
  }
  ```

Class to load and parse the package definition file, determine the
modules deployed and the changes to be made.
